perturbation.original_text: All such Contractors must process Merchant applications and transactions exclusively through Network 1. Network 1 consents to waive said exclusivity requirement with respect to specific Merchants in the event Network 1 (i) is unable to process for such specific Merchant, and (ii) the declined Merchant is not accepted for processing by a provider that Network 1 designates for specific Merchant's that are declined by Network 1 ("B Bank Source").
perturbation.changed_text: All such Contractors may process Merchant applications and transactions through any processor, regardless of Network 1's capabilities or designations. Network 1 retains the right to approve or deny specific Merchants at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that Contractors process transactions exclusively through Network 1, with a limited exception for Merchants Network 1 cannot process and that the "B Bank Source" declines. The modified text contradicts this by allowing Contractors to process transactions through any processor, effectively removing the exclusivity requirement. This directly contradicts the initial statement in 1.01 that they must process exclusively through Network 1, and Network 1 having "B Bank Source".
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONTRACTORS. Affiliate shall use its best efforts to market and sell to commercial businesses the Services of Network 1 and Network 1's subsidiaries and to locate individuals who are willing and capable of acting as Contractors of Network 1 and Affiliate subject to the approval of all such individuals by Network 1 as set out in Section 2.01 [CONTRACTORS]. All such Contractors may process Merchant applications and transactions through any processor, regardless of Network 1's capabilities or designations. Network 1 retains the right to approve or deny specific Merchants at its sole discretion. Affiliate shall disclose to Network 1 all information known to Affiliate with respect to the background, character, employment history, business experience and other information regarding each prospective Contractor relevant to Network 1's determination whether to accept or not accept each prospective Contractor.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated exclusive processing through Network 1, while the changed text permits processing through any processor. The contradicted text contains the changed text, creating a direct conflict with the initial requirement of exclusivity. The phrase 'All such Contractors may process Merchant applications and transactions through any processor, regardless of Network 1's capabilities or designations' contradicts the implicit exclusivity initially required.

perturbation.original_text: Network 1 agrees to make reasonable efforts to review and approve or decline all Merchant Agreements submitted to Network 1.
perturbation.changed_text: Network 1 has no obligation to review or respond to any Merchant Agreements submitted by Affiliate. Approval or declination of Merchant Agreements is solely at Network 1's discretion, and Network 1 is not obligated to provide a reason for any decision.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in 1.05 states that Network 1 agrees to make reasonable efforts to review and approve or decline all Merchant Agreements. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating that Network 1 has no obligation to review or respond to any agreements submitted by the Affiliate. This introduces uncertainty about Network 1's responsibilities and liabilities concerning Merchant Agreements. By stating Network 1 has no obligation, it creates an in-text contradiction about reviewing any merchant agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NETWORK 1 TO APPROVE ALL MERCHANTS AGREEMENTS. Affiliate agrees to submit all Merchant Agreements procured by Affiliate in accordance with this Agreement to Network 1. Network 1 agrees to review and approve or decline all Merchant Agreements submitted by Affiliate to Network 1 in accordance with Network 1's approval policy. Affiliate acknowledges that approval of a Merchant creates a customer relationship between Bank and the Merchant, which involves, among other things, the collection and disbursement of funds to process and settle Merchant Transactions. Bank and Network 1 shall, in its sole discretion, make the final decision as to whether or not approve or decline any Merchant Agreement submitted to Bank in accordance with this Agreement. Network 1 has no obligation to review or respond to any Merchant Agreements submitted by Affiliate. Approval or declination of Merchant Agreements is solely at Network 1's discretion, and Network 1 is not obligated to provide a reason for any decision.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Network 1 agrees to make reasonable efforts to review Merchant Agreements. The changed text directly states that Network 1 has no obligation to review these agreements. This creates a direct contradiction with the original text in the contract regarding Network 1's responsibilities concerning Merchant Agreements. The contradicted text reaffirms the initial agreement to review, further emphasizing the direct conflict, as well as the text after the changed text, which makes the modified text contradictory. This fits the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Affiliate acknowledges and agrees that it has no claims or rights of ownership over Merchants and subscribing businesses, Unless ISO agreement is consummated or Network 1 does not approve Affiliate or Network 1 cancels Affiliate and that it is a service provider to a customer of Network 1 (namely Merchants and subscribing businesses). In such case, Affiliate shall have the right to Transfer accounts in accordance with Section 2.03 (b) [RETENTION OF CONTRACTORS] and 2.03 (c).
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate unconditionally and irrevocably owns all Merchants and subscribing businesses sourced under this agreement, regardless of whether an ISO agreement is consummated or the status of Network 1's approval of Affiliate.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Affiliate has no rights of ownership over merchants except under specific conditions. The changed text states Affiliate unconditionally owns the merchants, in direct contradiction of the prior clause. This creates uncertainty about who owns the merchant relationships and introduces direct conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AMOUNT. As compensation for Affiliate's services hereunder, Network 1, or an affiliate, shall pay to Affiliate the following (the "Affiliate's Fee"): A. The surplus funding amount after costs noted in Exhibit A based on all Merchant applications obtained for Equipment and Products sold or leased by Affiliate or Contractors located by Affiliate pursuant to Section 1.01 [CONTRACTORS]. Affiliate shall receive the buy rate with revenue share as noted in Exhibit A. B. Affiliate acknowledges and agrees that all merchants and subscribing businesses referred by Affiliate are a party to a contract with Network 1 and it's Member bank and that Network 1 and the Member bank has a direct contractual relationship with the merchants and subscribing businesses. Network 1 acknowledges and agrees that Affiliate has certain rights to residual revenue as specifically defined in this agreement. Affiliate unconditionally and irrevocably owns all Merchants and subscribing businesses sourced under this agreement, regardless of whether an ISO agreement is consummated or the status of Network 1's approval of Affiliate. In such case, Affiliate shall have the right to Transfer accounts in accordance with Section 2.03 (b) [RETENTION OF CONTRACTORS] and 2.03 (c). The Affiliate's Fees outlined in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein shall be subject to modification by Network 1, at its discretion, upon the giving of thirty-days (30) prior written notification.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Affiliate unconditionally owns all Merchants, while the contradicted text includes the statement 'Affiliate acknowledges and agrees that all merchants and subscribing businesses referred by Affiliate are a party to a contract with Network 1 and it's Member bank and that Network 1 and the Member bank has a direct contractual relationship with the merchants and subscribing businesses' suggesting Network 1 has a direct contractual relationship with the merchants, thus contradicting the notion of the Affiliate unconditionally owning them. Furthermore, the original text also states the Affiliate has no rights of ownership over merchants except under specific conditions, creating a direct contradiction with the changed text.
